CheckTitle: AI agent roles must not use inline IAM policies
CheckType: Software and Configuration Checks/AI Agent Security Best Practices
Description: **AI Agent Permissions**. Checks that AI agent IAM roles and service accounts do not have inline policies attached. Inline policies are embedded directly in an IAM identity, do not appear in the managed-policy inventory, and are not subject to standard versioning or approval workflows.
Risk: Inline policies are invisible to policy-lifecycle tooling (Access Analyzer, Config rules, CSPM). They can be silently added or modified without triggering change-management controls, allowing an attacker with `iam:PutRolePolicy` to expand permissions without detection.

Code.Other:
1. List inline policies: `aws iam list-role-policies --role-name <role>`.
2. Retrieve each inline policy: `aws iam get-role-policy --role-name <role> --policy-name <name>`.
3. Create equivalent customer-managed policies with `aws iam create-policy`.
4. Attach the managed policies: `aws iam attach-role-policy`.
5. Delete the inline policies: `aws iam delete-role-policy`.
6. Verify the role's effective permissions are unchanged.

Recommendation.Text: Convert all inline policies to versioned customer-managed policies. Enforce the prohibition on inline policies via an SCP or IAM permission boundary that denies `iam:PutRolePolicy`. Audit for inline policy additions with AWS Config rule `iam-no-inline-policy-check`.

Notes: ASPM-014 check from the Prowler ASPM check suite.
